label: 115_ieepa_tariffs_tariff_section 301
description: The International Emergency Economic Powers Act's authority for imposing tariffs was challenged in Supreme Court cases, leading to rulings that limited the president’s power. Tariffs under this act have been a key tool in trade policy but now face stricter legal scrutiny.

earliest_articles.title: Evaluating Trump’s trade policies
earliest_articles.excerpt: It would be a grave mistake for American voters to underestimate the power of the US presidency – or to exaggerate the ability of checks and balances to rein in radical policies. Republican nominee Donald J Trump has made headlines threatening to pull out of the WTO (Mount 2016), renegotiate the North-American Free…

earliest_articles.title: Trump Gets an Economics Lesson from Harley-Davidson
earliest_articles.excerpt: Angered by Harley-Davidson’s response to punitive tariffs—namely, produce “hogs” abroad for foreign buyers—President Donald Trump tweeted that he would tax the company “like never before.” Over the past century, Congress has delegated to the president enormous swaths of its constitutional power to impose tariffs. But…

earliest_articles.title: Navarro Asks Congress to Give Trump Absolute Authority over the US Tariff Schedule
earliest_articles.excerpt: White House adviser Peter Navarro recently urged Congress to pass the “Reciprocal Trade Act,” drafted by Representative Sean Duffy (R-WI) at the behest of the White House. The bill would give Trump unfettered discretion to raise US tariffs against imports from countries that impose higher duties than existing US…

newest_articles.title: Will Trump's third attempt to impose tariffs survive in the courts?
newest_articles.excerpt: President Donald Trump's use of tariffs have lost repeatedly in the courts—first the president's use of emergency authority was condemned by the Court of International Trade, the Federal Circuit, and the Supreme Court, and second his use of temporary balance of payments authority was rejected by the Court of…

newest_articles.title: Why do Trump's tariffs have such staying power?
newest_articles.excerpt: Get used to President Donald Trump's comprehensive tariffs. Though the president's across-the-board tariffs imposed under his emergency powers were invalidated by the Supreme Court in February, the new more specific tariffs imposed in their place are likely to remain in place at least until a new administration takes…

newest_articles.title: Trump's latest tariffs in court: Are they about to be blocked?
newest_articles.excerpt: Soon after the Supreme Court struck down one set of President Donald Trump's tariffs in February, he imposed a second set that now faces legal challenges. The Court of International Trade (CIT) recently heard oral arguments in two cases on plaintiffs' requests for an injunction preventing the United States from…

central_articles.title: Legal and economic aspects of the Supreme Court’s upcoming tariff decisions
central_articles.excerpt: Among the numerous legal challenges to the actions taken by the Trump administration, two cases the Supreme Court will hear in November have been top of mind for many in the business community and for investors. Both involve the authority of President Trump to impose tariffs under the International Economic Emergency…

central_articles.title: Brookings experts on the Supreme Court’s tariff decision
central_articles.excerpt: On February 20, 2026, in Learning Resources Inc. v. Trump, the Supreme Court ruled that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) does not authorize the president to impose sweeping, open-ended tariffs—striking down the legal foundation for a central pillar of the administration’s trade strategy. The…

central_articles.title: Why does the executive branch have so much power over tariffs?
central_articles.excerpt: In recent years, Presidents Trump and Biden have exercised an aggressive level of executive authority to raise tariffs on imported goods. Most of these tariffs have been implemented under broad authority delegated by Congress to combat unfair practices or national security. As a result, tariff revenue as a share of…
